components.docs: A persistent inline status message with token-backed tone semantics.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
show a persistent inline message about the state of a page or
section, such as a failed save, completed operation, or service notice, that
remains until the condition is resolved or the person dismisses it. Express
severity with `tone`, never custom styling.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
transient confirmations that expire on their own belong to
the future `ki-toast`; tiny status descriptors attached to another element
belong to `ki-badge`; blocking decisions belong to `ki-dialog`; inline
field-level validation belongs to the form control.

Assistive technology note: alerts that must be announced should be inserted
dynamically, or re-shown by clearing `dismissed`; alerts present at initial
page load are exposed with their role but platform announcement is not
guaranteed.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: - Message body. It lives inside the live-region boundary.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: alert - Outer alert surface: tone background, border, radius, and padding.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: heading - Optional emphasized heading, rendered only when non-empty.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: message - Message body wrapper around the default slot.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: dismiss - Native dismiss button, rendered only when dismissible.

props.name: dismissLabel
props.docs:
Accessible name for the dismiss button. Override for localization; the
default English string is the component's only built-in user-visible text.

When to use: provide a localized action name whenever the document language
is not English.
When NOT to use: do not put the alert message here; use the default slot.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: 'Dismiss'

props.name: dismissed
props.docs:
Reflected dismissed state. User dismissal sets it; applications may also
set or clear it. While true, the host remains in the document but renders no
alert subtree and leaves the accessibility tree. Clearing it re-shows the
alert and creates a dynamic live-region appearance.

When to use: persist or restore acknowledgement state from application
data.
When NOT to use: do not listen for programmatic changes as dismissal
events; `ki-dismiss` is only for user activation.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: dismissible
props.docs:
Renders one native dismiss button when true. The button sits outside the
live-region boundary, so its accessible name is not announced as part of
the alert message. When false, the alert adds no tab stop.

When to use: allow a person to acknowledge and clear a persistent message.
When NOT to use: do not use dismissible for auto-expiring messages; that is
future `ki-toast` behavior.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: heading
props.docs:
Optional emphasized text rendered before the message inside the live
region. Empty strings render no heading. The heading is a `strong`
element, not a document heading, so it never changes page outline.

When to use: add a short label when it helps identify the status message.
When NOT to use: do not use heading for page structure; use a real heading
outside the alert when the document needs one.

props.name: tone
props.docs:
Semantic intent for visual styling and live-region urgency. `danger` and
`warning` expose `role="alert"`; `neutral`, `success`, `info`, absent, and
unrecognized values expose `role="status"`. Unknown values keep rendering
and fall back to the neutral token matrix by CSS construction.

When to use: choose the tone that describes the page or section state.
When NOT to use: do not use tone for layout, density, or filled-vs-outlined
styling; those are token/theme decisions.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: 'neutral'

events.docs:
Fired once after the user dismisses the alert — emitted after the alert is
hidden and focus has been handed to the next control. `detail` is `null`
and the event is not cancelable (the alert is already gone when it runs).
When to use: record acknowledgement, or advance an application flow after a
user closes the alert.
When NOT to use: do not treat it as a veto point, and do not expect it for
programmatic `dismissed` changes — it fires only for user activation.

slots.docs: Message body. It lives inside the live-region boundary.

parts.name: alert
parts.docs: Outer alert surface: tone background, border, radius, and padding.

parts.name: dismiss
parts.docs: Native dismiss button, rendered only when dismissible.

parts.name: heading
parts.docs: Optional emphasized heading, rendered only when non-empty.

parts.name: message
parts.docs: Message body wrapper around the default slot.

components.docs: A static, non-interactive status pill.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
annotate an entity with short status text (a state, a
category) whose meaning is carried by the label itself; the tone color
only reinforces the text, it never replaces it.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
feedback that must be announced (that is
ki-alert's job — the badge has no live region), an interactive
chip, filter or button, empty content (the label IS the meaning), or a
notification-counter overlay (a future, separate concern).

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: - The label: short status text, the sole carrier of meaning.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text:
badge - The pill: background, foreground, border, radius, metrics
and typography.

props.name: size
props.docs:
Metric scale (`--ki-badge-{size}-*` tokens). An unrecognized value
falls back to the `md` metrics the same way.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: 'md'

props.name: tone
props.docs:
Semantic intent, never appearance: each tone resolves its colors from
the `--ki-badge-{tone}-*` tokens. An unrecognized value matches no
style selector, so the badge keeps the neutral appearance (fallback by
CSS construction — no validation code).

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: 'neutral'

slots.docs: The label: short status text, the sole carrier of meaning.

parts.name: badge
parts.docs:
The pill: background, foreground, border, radius, metrics
and typography.

components.docs: A token-styled action button with native button semantics.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
trigger the single main action of a view, supporting actions
in descending hierarchy, or confirming/destructive actions through tone.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
navigation, icon-only actions, persistent toggles, or
loading/progress semantics.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: - Label content. This is the accessible name source.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: start - Leading icon or media. Follows writing direction.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: end - Trailing icon or media. Follows writing direction.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: button - Internal native button.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: label - Label wrapper around the default slot.

props.name: disabled
props.docs:
Prevents activation, removes the button from keyboard reach, and exposes
the unavailable state through the internal native button.
When NOT to use: do not use disabled for pending/loading semantics.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: name
props.docs:
Form-data key contributed when this button submits its form.
When NOT to use: omit when no submitter value should be sent.

props.name: size
props.docs:
Token-backed button size. Every size keeps at least the minimum pointer
target; choose the size that matches the density of the surrounding UI.
When NOT to use: do not use `ki-button` for icon-only compact controls.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: 'md'

props.name: tone
props.docs:
Semantic intent for the action, independent of hierarchy. Use `success`
for confirming actions and `danger` for destructive actions.
When NOT to use: do not use tone for visual hierarchy; use `variant`.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: 'neutral'

props.name: type
props.docs:
Native form action type: `submit` submits the owning form (running
constraint validation and contributing `name`/`value` to the form data),
`reset` restores field defaults, `button` never touches the form.
Cancel a submission from the form's `submit` event (`preventDefault()`);
unlike a native button, `preventDefault()` on the `click` event does not
cancel it. During submission `event.submitter` is a transient native
button carrying this element's `name`/`value`, not the `ki-button` host.
When NOT to use: use `button` when the action must never submit a form.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: 'submit'

props.name: value
props.docs:
Form-data value paired with `name` when this button submits its form.
When NOT to use: omit when the default empty submitter value is intended.

props.name: variant
props.docs:
Visual hierarchy for the action. Use `primary` for the single main action
in a view and lower-emphasis variants for supporting actions.
When NOT to use: do not use variant to signal success or danger; use
`tone` for intent.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: 'secondary'

slots.docs: Label content. This is the accessible name source.

slots.name: end
slots.docs: Trailing icon or media. Follows writing direction.

slots.name: start
slots.docs: Leading icon or media. Follows writing direction.

parts.name: button
parts.docs: Internal native button.

parts.name: label
parts.docs: Label wrapper around the default slot.

components.docs: A non-interactive card surface for grouping related content.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
group related media, heading, supporting text and actions into
one scannable surface visually distinct from the page; fill any subset of
regions. Supply the heading element yourself in the `header` slot — plain
text slotted there carries no heading semantics for assistive technology.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
as a button or link target, form control, fieldset, page
landmark, section replacement or nested card. For an interactive card, slot
the button or link INSIDE a region (whole-card interactivity is a future
feature, not this component).

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: media - Leading visual region for an image, video or illustration.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: header - Title region. The author supplies the heading element at the surrounding document level; plain text here carries no heading semantics.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: - Body region for supporting text or arbitrary composed content.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: footer - Closing region for actions such as `ki-button`; no dedicated actions slot exists in v1.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: card - Card surface: background, border, radius, elevation and region gap.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: media - Media region wrapper.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: header - Header region wrapper.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: body - Body region wrapper around the default slot.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: footer - Footer region wrapper.

slots.docs: Body region for supporting text or arbitrary composed content.

slots.name: footer
slots.docs: Closing region for actions such as `ki-button`; no dedicated actions slot exists in v1.

slots.name: header
slots.docs: Title region. The author supplies the heading element at the surrounding document level; plain text here carries no heading semantics.

slots.name: media
slots.docs: Leading visual region for an image, video or illustration.

parts.name: body
parts.docs: Body region wrapper around the default slot.

parts.name: card
parts.docs: Card surface: background, border, radius, elevation and region gap.

parts.name: footer
parts.docs: Footer region wrapper.

parts.name: header
parts.docs: Header region wrapper.

parts.name: media
parts.docs: Media region wrapper.

components.docs: A form-associated checkbox for selecting independent options.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
selecting one or more independent options that a form submits
later, including a "select all" parent that presents partial selection with
`indeterminate`. Always provide a visible label in the default slot.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
a single mutually exclusive choice, an immediate on/off
effect, triggering an action, unlabeled/icon-only usage, or
`checked="false"` to mean unchecked. Boolean attributes use presence
semantics; omit `checked` to express unchecked.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text:
- Visible label content. This is the accessible name source and a
native activation surface.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: control - Visual checkbox box, border, focus ring and currentColor marks.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: label - Label wrapper around the default slot.

props.name: checked
props.docs:
Live binary selection state. User activation by pointer, slotted label or
Space toggles it with native checkbox parity and emits composed `input`
before composed `change`. Boolean presence semantics apply:
`checked="false"` still renders checked; omit the attribute to express
unchecked. Programmatic assignment is silent.
When NOT to use: do not treat this reflected attribute as a native
`defaultChecked`; reset uses the baseline captured at form association.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: disabled
props.docs:
Prevents activation, removes the checkbox from keyboard reach, exposes the
unavailable state, and excludes it from form data.
When NOT to use: do not use disabled for validation errors or pending
state.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: indeterminate
props.docs:
Presentation-only mixed state. It renders the dash mark, is forwarded to
the internal native input for mixed assistive-technology exposure, and
never changes the submitted value. Any user toggle clears it and removes
the reflected attribute.
When NOT to use: do not submit or persist `indeterminate` as a third
value; model data remains binary through `checked`.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: name
props.docs:
Form-data key contributed when the checkbox is checked.
When NOT to use: omit when the checkbox should not submit a value.

props.name: required
props.docs:
Requires the checkbox to be checked before form submission can proceed.
The invalid appearance appears after a blocked submission attempt or an
invalidating user toggle, never on initial render.
When NOT to use: do not use required to express group-level rules; compose
those at the form/application layer.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: value
props.docs:
Form-data value paired with `name` when checked. If omitted, the submitted
value is `on`, matching native checkbox behavior.
When NOT to use: do not encode the unchecked state here; unchecked
checkboxes contribute no form entry.

slots.docs:
Visible label content. This is the accessible name source and a
native activation surface.

parts.name: control
parts.docs: Visual checkbox box, border, focus ring and currentColor marks.

parts.name: label
parts.docs: Label wrapper around the default slot.

components.docs: A modal dialog for one interrupting decision or short focused task.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
destructive confirmations, blocking choices, and brief
critical input that must be resolved before returning to the page. Always
provide a `heading`, place actions in the `footer` slot, wire each footer
action to `close()`, and in destructive confirmations put `autofocus` on
the least destructive action.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
non-blocking feedback (`ki-alert`, future `ki-toast`),
supplementary hints (`ki-tooltip`), long forms or multi-step flows
(navigate or use a future full-screen variant), menus, or pickers.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: - Dialog body content.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: footer - Dialog actions; applications wire every action to `close()`.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: dialog - Internal native dialog surface.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: heading - Visible h2 title, rendered only when `heading` is non-empty.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: body - Scrollable body region.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: footer - Action row, collapsed when empty.

props.name: closeOnBackdrop
props.docs:
Opts into backdrop light-dismiss. Omit this attribute for critical
confirmations; `close-on-backdrop="false"` still enables it.
When to use: low-risk dialogs where an outside click may safely dismiss.
When NOT to use: destructive confirmations or decisions that should not be
lost to a stray click; omit the attribute entirely rather than setting it
to `"false"`.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: heading
props.docs:
Visible dialog title and accessible-name source. Always provide a heading;
an empty value intentionally leaves the native dialog unnamed.
When to use: name the interrupting decision, for example "Delete
account?". When NOT to use: do not omit it for production dialogs; APG
modal dialogs require an accessible name.

props.name: open
props.docs:
Reflected live modal state. Add it or call `show()` to open; remove it or
call `close()` to close. When open, the native dialog enters the top layer
and the page behind is inert.
When to use: bind application state to the dialog's modal lifecycle.
When NOT to use: do not set the internal native `<dialog open>` attribute;
the host attribute is the only public source of truth.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

methods.name: close
methods.docs:
Closes the dialog and reports `method`. No-op when already closed.
Equivalent to removing the host `open` attribute. Footer actions never
close automatically; wire them to this method.
When to use: resolve footer actions, programmatic dismissals, and
application-controlled cancellation. When NOT to use: do not use for
Escape or backdrop bookkeeping; those paths set their own close reasons.

methods.name: show
methods.docs:
Opens the dialog modally. No-op when already open. Equivalent to adding
the host `open` attribute.
When to use: call from the invoker that should receive focus again after
close. When NOT to use: do not call repeatedly to refresh content; update
slotted content directly while open.

events.docs:
Post-close notification for every close path. Footer actions report
`method` when they call `close()`, Escape reports `escape`, and opt-in
backdrop dismissal reports `backdrop`.
When to use: update application state after the dialog is already closed
and focus has returned through the native mechanism.
When NOT to use: do not expect this event to veto closing; it is not
cancelable in v1.

slots.docs: Dialog body content.

slots.name: footer
slots.docs: Dialog actions; applications wire every action to `close()`.

parts.name: body
parts.docs: Scrollable body region.

parts.name: dialog
parts.docs: Internal native dialog surface.

parts.name: footer
parts.docs: Action row, collapsed when empty.

parts.name: heading
parts.docs: Visible h2 title, rendered only when `heading` is non-empty.

components.docs: A token-styled single-line text field with native input semantics.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
collect one line of free text from a person, always with a
visible `label`; choose the `type` and `autocomplete` that match the entry
purpose.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
multiline text, predefined choices, boolean state, numeric
stepper entry, or placeholder-only labeling.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: start - Leading icon or text affix inside the field. Follows writing direction.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: end - Trailing icon or text affix inside the field. Follows writing direction.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: field - Enclosure wrapper for background, border, radius and focus ring.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: input - Internal native input that owns entry text, caret and selection.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: label - Visible label that provides the accessible name.

props.name: autocomplete
props.docs:
Native autocomplete detail token forwarded to the internal input.
When NOT to use: omit when no autofill entry purpose is known.

props.name: disabled
props.docs:
Prevents editing, removes the field from keyboard reach and exposes the
unavailable state through the internal native input.
When NOT to use: do not use disabled for readonly reference values.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: label
props.docs:
Visible label rendered next to the entry area and used as the accessible
name. This is mandatory for valid usage.
When NOT to use: never use `placeholder` as a label substitute.

props.name: name
props.docs:
Form-data key for the submitted value.
When NOT to use: omit when the field must not contribute named form data.

props.name: placeholder
props.docs:
Hint shown when the field is empty.
When NOT to use: do not use placeholder as the accessible name.

props.name: readonly
props.docs:
Makes the value focusable and selectable while rejecting edits.
When NOT to use: use `disabled` when the value must be unavailable and
excluded from forms.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: required
props.docs:
Marks the field as required for native constraint validation.
When NOT to use: do not use required on optional fields.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: type
props.docs:
Entry kind with native single-line input semantics. Unknown runtime
values fall back to `text`; `number` is not a v1 input kind.
When NOT to use: use future numeric controls for locale-aware number entry.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: 'text'

props.name: value
props.docs:
Live text value. The attribute declares the initial default; the property
is the current value and programmatic assignments are silent.
Deviation from native (deliberate, research D2): assigning the ATTRIBUTE
programmatically also replaces the displayed value, silently — native
inputs would keep the user's dirty value. Form reset restores the
attribute's current value.
When NOT to use: do not observe user edits by polling; listen for `input`
and `change` (both re-dispatched composed across the shadow boundary).

slots.name: end
slots.docs: Trailing icon or text affix inside the field. Follows writing direction.

slots.name: start
slots.docs: Leading icon or text affix inside the field. Follows writing direction.

parts.name: field
parts.docs: Enclosure wrapper for background, border, radius and focus ring.

parts.name: input
parts.docs: Internal native input that owns entry text, caret and selection.

parts.name: label
parts.docs: Visible label that provides the accessible name.

components.docs:
A non-interactive vertical list container for read-only collections of
similar entries composed with `ki-list-item` children.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
settings, contacts, results or activity feeds where each item
composes leading media, primary text, optional secondary text and trailing
meta or a slotted control.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
menus, selectable option lists, tabular data, navigation,
whole-item clickable rows or lone items outside a list.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: - `ki-list-item` children. Other children are unsupported.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text:
list - List surface that owns background, padding, item gap and
between-item divider styling.

slots.docs: `ki-list-item` children. Other children are unsupported.

parts.name: list
parts.docs:
List surface that owns background, padding, item gap and
between-item divider styling.

components.docs:
A non-interactive item inside `ki-list`, composed from leading media,
primary text, optional secondary text and trailing media or meta.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
only as a child of `ki-list`, for one read-only entry in a
similar vertical collection.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
outside `ki-list`, as a menu item, selectable option,
tabular row, navigation link or whole-item clickable control.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: start - Leading media such as an icon, avatar or image.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: - Primary text line.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text:
secondary - Supporting text below the primary line. Its presence
selects the multi-line min-height token.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: end - Trailing media, meta text or a slotted control.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: item - Item row surface and layout container.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: start - Leading region wrapper.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: content - Text column containing primary and secondary lines.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: end - Trailing region wrapper.

slots.docs: Primary text line.

slots.name: end
slots.docs: Trailing media, meta text or a slotted control.

slots.name: secondary
slots.docs:
Supporting text below the primary line. Its presence
selects the multi-line min-height token.

slots.name: start
slots.docs: Leading media such as an icon, avatar or image.

parts.name: content
parts.docs: Text column containing primary and secondary lines.

parts.name: end
parts.docs: Trailing region wrapper.

parts.name: item
parts.docs: Item row surface and layout container.

parts.name: start
parts.docs: Leading region wrapper.

components.docs: A declarative data option rendered by its owning `ki-select`.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
declare one choice inside a `ki-select`; its text is the
human-facing label and its `value` is the submitted value.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
`ki-option` standalone, authoring selection
on an option, or expecting it to paint its own row.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: - Text label mirrored by the owning select.

props.name: disabled
props.docs:
Makes this option unavailable. Disabled options cannot be selected, are
skipped by keyboard highlight, and are exposed unavailable by the select.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: value
props.docs:
Submission and selection value for this option. When omitted, the value
falls back to the trimmed option text, matching native `<option>` parity.
When NOT to use: do not use this as selection state; set `ki-select.value`.

slots.docs: Text label mirrored by the owning select.

components.docs:
A token-styled, non-interactive progress indicator for known or unknown
duration work.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
communicate advancement of an ongoing task such as upload,
download, installation or multi-step processing. Use `value`/`max` when
the completed fraction is known; use `indeterminate` when work is ongoing
but its duration cannot be measured, including loading-indicator use cases.
Choose `linear` in page flows and lists, and `circular` in compact or
centered placements. Always set `label` to what is progressing.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
static measurements within a known range such as disk
usage or scores (gauge/meter), step-by-step wizard navigation (stepper),
skeleton placeholders while content loads, or operations that finish in
under about one second.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: track - The full channel or ring: track ink and radius/stroke geometry.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text:
indicator - The advancing fill or arc: indicator ink and indeterminate
animation.

props.name: indeterminate
props.docs:
Unknown-duration mode. When set, no completed fraction or current value is
exposed. Its motion is declared only when reduced motion is not requested.
When to use: show ongoing work whose duration or total cannot be measured.
When NOT to use: do not use for known fractions; use `value` and `max`.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: label
props.docs:
Accessible name applied to the internal progressbar. Always set this to
what is progressing, such as "Uploading report.pdf". Without it the
element renders but exposes no accessible name.
When NOT to use: do not use a generic label such as "Loading" when the
task can be named more specifically.

props.name: max
props.docs:
Total amount. Non-finite, zero or negative values normalize to `100` for
presentation and ARIA.
When to use: set when a determinate task's total is not 100.
When NOT to use: omit for conventional percentage-style progress.

props.name: shape
props.docs:
Structural presentation. Use `linear` in page flows and lists; use
`circular` in compact or centered placements. Unknown values render linear.
When NOT to use: do not use shape to encode semantic status or task intent.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: 'linear'

props.name: value

parts.name: indicator
parts.docs:
The advancing fill or arc: indicator ink and indeterminate
animation.

parts.name: track
parts.docs: The full channel or ring: track ink and radius/stroke geometry.

components.docs: One option in a token-styled radio group.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
place inside `ki-radio-group` when a person must choose
exactly one of a small visible set.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
`ki-radio` standalone, multiple selection,
or authored selection state; set the parent group's `value` instead.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: - Option label. This is the accessible name and activation surface.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: control - Visual ring and selected dot.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: label - Wrapper around the option label.

props.name: disabled
props.docs:
Prevents this option from being selected or focused. Disabled options are
skipped by group arrow navigation and omitted from form submission when
selected before becoming disabled.
When NOT to use: do not use disabled as a temporary loading state.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: value
props.docs:
Submission value projected by the parent `ki-radio-group` when this option
is selected. Omit for native radio parity with value `"on"`.
When NOT to use: do not use `value` to author selection; set the group's
`value` property or attribute.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: 'on'

slots.docs: Option label. This is the accessible name and activation surface.

parts.name: control
parts.docs: Visual ring and selected dot.

parts.name: label
parts.docs: Wrapper around the option label.

components.docs:
A token-styled radio group that owns selection, keyboard coordination and
form participation for slotted `ki-radio` options.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
a person must choose exactly one of a small set of mutually
exclusive options that should all be visible at once.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
many options or tight space (use `ki-select`), independent
on/off settings (use `ki-checkbox` or `ki-switch`), multiple selection, or
authored selection on options; set this group's `value` instead.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: - `ki-radio` options. Document order is navigation order.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: label - Visible group label and accessible-name source.

props.name: disabled
props.docs:
Makes the whole group unavailable, skips it in Tab order and removes its
form entry.
When NOT to use: do not use disabled for pending/loading semantics.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: label
props.docs:
Visible label and accessible-name source for the radiogroup.
When NOT to use: do not omit it; unlabeled groups fail accessibility gates.

props.name: name
props.docs:
Form-data key for the selected option's value. Omit when the group should
not contribute a form entry.
When NOT to use: do not put `name` on `ki-radio` options; their internal
native inputs are intentionally unnamed.

props.name: required
props.docs:
Requires one selected option for form submission. The group uses platform
`valueMissing` from its internal native radio inputs.
When NOT to use: do not use required when no answer is acceptable.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: value
props.docs:
Projection of the current selection. The initial attribute selects the
first matching option; unmatched values leave the group unselected and
operable. Assigning the property updates selection silently.
When NOT to use: never author selection on `ki-radio`; set this value.

slots.docs: `ki-radio` options. Document order is navigation order.

parts.name: label
parts.docs: Visible group label and accessible-name source.

components.docs:
A form-associated select-only combobox for choosing one option from
declarative `ki-option` children.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
choose exactly one value from a known closed list, especially
when there are roughly five or more choices or space is limited.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
`ki-radio-group` for a few always-visible choices,
`ki-input` for free or searchable text, `ki-checkbox`/`ki-switch` for
binary decisions, or multiselect and command menus.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: - `ki-option` data children. They do not paint; rows are mirrored.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: label - Visible label and accessible-name source.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: trigger - Native button carrying the combobox role.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: value - Displayed selection or placeholder.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: indicator - Decorative dropdown indicator.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: listbox - Popup listbox surface.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: option - Mirrored option row rendered in this shadow root.

props.name: disabled
props.docs:
Prevents opening, removes the trigger from keyboard reach, and excludes
the select from form submission. Boolean presence semantics apply.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: label
props.docs:
Visible label and accessible-name source for the combobox trigger.
When NOT to use: do not omit it; unlabeled selects are invalid usage.

props.name: name
props.docs:
Form-data key used when a selected option contributes its value.
When NOT to use: omit it when the select should not submit data.

props.name: placeholder
props.docs:
Text shown while no option is selected.
When NOT to use: do not use it as a replacement for `label`.

props.name: required
props.docs:
Requires a non-empty submitted value. The platform validation message is
donated by a hidden native select.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: value
props.docs:
Live projection of the selected option value, or `""` when unselected.
Assigning it selects the first matching option silently; the attribute is
the reset/default declaration and is not updated by user commits.

slots.docs: `ki-option` data children. They do not paint; rows are mirrored.

parts.name: indicator
parts.docs: Decorative dropdown indicator.

parts.name: label
parts.docs: Visible label and accessible-name source.

parts.name: listbox
parts.docs: Popup listbox surface.

parts.name: option
parts.docs: Mirrored option row rendered in this shadow root.

parts.name: trigger
parts.docs: Native button carrying the combobox role.

parts.name: value
parts.docs: Displayed selection or placeholder.

components.docs: A token-styled switch for immediate on/off settings.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
binary settings whose change takes effect immediately, always
with a slotted label.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
selections collected for later form submission; use
ki-checkbox for recorded choices, ki-radio-group for mutually exclusive
choices, and ki-button for actions.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: - Label content. This is the accessible name source.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: track - On/off channel.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: thumb - Traveling handle.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: label - Label wrapper around the default slot.

props.name: checked
props.docs:
Live on/off state. Boolean presence semantics apply: any present
`checked` attribute value, including `checked="false"` or malformed
agent output, means on. Omit the attribute to express off.
When to use: set the initial on state for a setting that applies
immediately.
When NOT to use: do not use a switch for choices saved only on submit;
use ki-checkbox for that pattern.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: disabled
props.docs:
Prevents toggling, removes the switch from keyboard reach, excludes it
from form data, and exposes the unavailable state to assistive technology.
When to use: make a setting temporarily unavailable while preserving its
current state.
When NOT to use: do not use disabled for pending or loading states.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: name
props.docs:
Form-data key contributed while the switch is on.
When to use: include the immediate setting in native form data when on.
When NOT to use: omit when no form entry should be submitted.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: undefined

props.name: value
props.docs:
Form-data value submitted while on. Omit for native checkbox parity: the
submitted value defaults to `on`.
When to use: submit a domain-specific value instead of the default `on`.
When NOT to use: do not set a value to represent off; off contributes
nothing.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: 'on'

slots.docs: Label content. This is the accessible name source.

parts.name: label
parts.docs: Label wrapper around the default slot.

parts.name: thumb
parts.docs: Traveling handle.

parts.name: track
parts.docs: On/off channel.

components.docs: One selectable tab inside a `ki-tabs` view switcher.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
label one peer content view inside `ki-tabs`, with optional
`start` and `end` slot media.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
standalone, for form value
selection, or for page navigation; use the parent group's `value` instead
of authoring `selected`.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: - Tab label; this is the accessible name source.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: start - Leading icon or media. Follows writing direction.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: end - Trailing icon or media. Follows writing direction.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: tab - Tab surface.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: indicator - Decorative selected-tab marker.

props.name: disabled
props.docs:
Prevents selection by every modality and exposes the unavailable state.
Boolean presence semantics apply: `disabled="false"` is still disabled.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: selected
props.docs:
Output-only selected state written by `ki-tabs`. Set the group's `value`
to choose an initial tab; author-set `selected` is overwritten.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: value
props.docs:
Pairing identifier shared with a `ki-tab-panel`. The first tab with a
value owns it; later duplicates render but are not selectable.

slots.docs: Tab label; this is the accessible name source.

slots.name: end
slots.docs: Trailing icon or media. Follows writing direction.

slots.name: start
slots.docs: Leading icon or media. Follows writing direction.

parts.name: indicator
parts.docs: Decorative selected-tab marker.

parts.name: tab
parts.docs: Tab surface.

components.docs: One content view paired with a `ki-tab` inside `ki-tabs`.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
hold the content for one peer tab view, sharing `value` with
its `ki-tab`.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
standalone, as lazy mounting, or for page
navigation; orphan and duplicate panels are hidden by the parent group.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: - Panel content.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: panel - Panel surface.

props.name: value
props.docs:
Pairing identifier shared with a `ki-tab`. The first panel with a value
owns it; duplicate or orphan panels stay hidden.

slots.docs: Panel content.

parts.name: panel
parts.docs: Panel surface.

components.docs: A tab group for switching between peer content views.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
switch between small sets of peer views inside the same page,
with one visible panel at a time.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
selecting form values
(use `ki-radio-group`), page navigation (use links), step flows, or
standalone `ki-tab` / `ki-tab-panel` children outside this group.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text:
- `ki-tab` and `ki-tab-panel` children. Tabs are auto-assigned to an
internal named slot as managed output.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: tablist - Internal strip carrying the `tablist` role.

props.name: label
props.docs:
Accessible name for the tablist. Always provide one when multiple tab
groups may appear in a view. When NOT to use: do not use `label` as a
visible heading; render visible context in surrounding content.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: undefined

props.name: value

events.docs:
Fired once after a user-driven selection change from pointer or keyboard
navigation. `detail.value` is the resolved selected value and `value` is
already current when listeners run. Programmatic `value` writes and
first-render fallback are silent.

slots.docs:
`ki-tab` and `ki-tab-panel` children. Tabs are auto-assigned to an
internal named slot as managed output.

parts.name: tablist
parts.docs: Internal strip carrying the `tablist` role.

components.docs: A token-styled multiline text field with native form semantics.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
free-form text longer than one line, such as comments,
descriptions, messages, delivery notes, or addresses when paired with a
matching `autocomplete` purpose.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
single-line values (`ki-input`), constrained choices,
rich or formatted text editing, or search boxes.

Agent note: initial text is declared through the `value` attribute; element
text content is ignored. Enter inserts a line break and never submits the
enclosing form, the inverse of `ki-input`.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: field - The enclosure wrapper.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: textarea - Internal native multiline control.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: label - Rendered visible label and accessible-name source.

props.name: autocomplete
props.docs:
Autofill detail token forwarded to the native textarea.
When to use: expose entry purpose such as `street-address` when available.
When NOT to use: omit when no valid autofill purpose applies.

props.name: disabled
props.docs:
Disables editing, focus, validation and form-data contribution.
When to use: make a field temporarily unavailable. When NOT to use: do not
use disabled for readonly review text that should submit.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: label
props.docs:
Visible label rendered by the component and used as the accessible name.
When to use: always provide a concise label for the requested long-form
text. When NOT to use: do not substitute placeholder text for the label.

props.name: name
props.docs:
Form-data key used when the textarea submits with a form.
When to use: provide for fields whose text must be included in FormData.
When NOT to use: omit for display-only or client-only fields.

props.name: placeholder
props.docs:
Hint shown while the textarea is empty.
When to use: add an example or short formatting hint. When NOT to use: do
not use placeholder as the accessible name or required instruction.

props.name: readonly
props.docs:
Makes the textarea focusable and selectable while rejecting edits.
When to use: show submitted or policy text that should still be included
in form data. When NOT to use: do not use readonly to remove a field from
submission; use disabled.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: required
props.docs:
Requires a non-empty value before form submission.
When to use: mark mandatory long-form text. When NOT to use: do not pair
with `readonly` expecting it to block; readonly fields are validation
exempt like native textareas.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: false

props.name: rows
props.docs:
Visible line count. Invalid, non-numeric, zero, or negative values fall
back to 2; no auto-grow or user resize handle exists in v1.
When to use: set the stable multiline height needed by the layout. When
NOT to use: do not use rows as a responsive size axis.

props.name: value
props.docs:
Live current text. The `value` attribute declares the reset default;
element text content is ignored. Programmatic assignments replace the
display and emit no events.
When to use: preload or read free-form text, line breaks included. When
NOT to use: do not put initial text between the element tags.

parts.name: field
parts.docs: The enclosure wrapper.

parts.name: label
parts.docs: Rendered visible label and accessible-name source.

parts.name: textarea
parts.docs: Internal native multiline control.

components.docs: A transient, text-only description bubble for one slotted trigger.

docsTags.name: whenToUse
docsTags.text:
add a brief clarifying hint for an icon-only, abbreviated, or
otherwise ambiguous control when the same information is discoverable
elsewhere in the interface.

docsTags.name: whenNotToUse
docsTags.text:
essential or unique information in a tooltip; interactive
or rich content in a tooltip; form validation messages, disabled controls,
or touch-primary flows. Use visible layout text or a future `ki-popover`
pattern for those cases.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text:
- Exactly one interactive trigger. The component reflects `label` to
the trigger's `aria-description`.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: tooltip - The non-focusable tooltip bubble.

props.name: label
props.docs:
The entire tooltip content. The string is reflected to the slotted
trigger's accessible description without changing its name. Empty or
whitespace-only labels render no tooltip and expose no description.
When to use: a short hint that clarifies the slotted trigger.
When NOT to use: never use `label` for essential information, rich
content, interactive content, validation messages, or information attached
to disabled controls; put that content in visible UI or a future popover.

props.name: placement
props.docs:
Preferred placement for the tooltip. The component may flip or clamp the
rendered placement to keep the bubble inside the viewport; unknown runtime
values fall back to `top`.
When NOT to use: do not depend on placement for meaning or reading order.

docsTags.name: default
docsTags.text: 'top'

slots.docs:
Exactly one interactive trigger. The component reflects `label` to
the trigger's `aria-description`.

parts.name: tooltip
parts.docs: The non-focusable tooltip bubble.

src/components/ki-dialog/ki-dialog.tsx::.docstring: Event payload for the post-close `ki-close` notification.
